authoritative_sources.title: 1302.12 Determining, verifying, and documenting eligibility
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for Head Start eligibility determination, evidence verification, and eligibility-record contents. It is directly authoritative for ERSEA enrollment work and provides concrete child-services documents and refusal triggers.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302 Subpart A—Eligibility, Recruitment, Selection, Enrollment, and Attendance
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Canonical public workflow reference for Head Start ERSEA stages, making it directly authoritative for intake, selection, enrollment, and attendance progression.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.13 Recruitment of children
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official regulation that requires active recruitment and application assistance for children in need, including children with disabilities, children experiencing homelessness, and children in foster care.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.16 Attendance
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official attendance rule for enrolled children that also governs conditional attendance for children experiencing homelessness and safety follow-up for absences, making it a direct lane control for enrollment readiness and record timing.

authoritative_sources.title: Addressing Vacant Slots Due to Chronic Absenteeism in Head Start Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHS instruction clarifying that funded enrollment must be maintained, open slots filled within 30 days, and reserved slots handled under explicit constraints. This directly shapes acceptance, waitlist, and slot-vacancy decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Head Start Health Services Year-at-a-Glance
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official operational guidance tying enrollment date to health-service consent, screening, physical exam, and immunization records, which are practical must-check artifacts for intake and start readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: FAQs About Serving Families Experiencing Homelessness
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHS FAQ guidance that clarifies priority, reserved-slot use, and acceptable homelessness verification behavior in enrollment workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Recruiting and Enrolling Children
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official disability-services orientation that explains how ERSEA and IDEA-linked prioritization intersect during recruiting and enrolling children with disabilities.

authoritative_sources.title: Child Care Financial Assistance Options
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care guidance on voucher, subsidy, and Head Start financial pathways. It is authoritative for distinguishing child care enrollment readiness from external funding approval.

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Child Care Licensing?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal child-care licensing guidance explaining licensed versus legally exempt settings, monitoring, health-safety minimums, and the licensing status checks that materially affect whether a child care placement can start.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Background Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal control guidance establishing who must have background checks and how often they must be renewed, which affects whether a chosen provider or room is actually enrollment-ready.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Qualifications and Required Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal child-care guidance describing training and qualification expectations by setting and age served, which materially changes whether a room or provider can accept a child safely.

authoritative_sources.title: Equal Access to Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal civil-rights guidance for child care admissions, prohibiting exclusion of children with disabilities based on assumptions and requiring individualized assessment and reasonable modifications within legal limits.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and child-identity normalization
workflow_stages.description: Validate who is requesting care, which child is in scope, the child’s age and program fit, and whether the intake is actually child-care enrollment work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility, funding, and priority-path verification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm income, public-assistance, homelessness, foster-care, disability-priority, subsidy, and funded-slot conditions before a start path is offered.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Health, consent, and provider-readiness artifact review
workflow_stages.description: Check health records, immunization timing, screening consent, pickup authorizations, licensing context, and provider readiness before execution transfer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Acceptance, pend, rejection, and routing
workflow_stages.description: Set a queue-state outcome and package a traceable next-step artifact to the correct owner when enrollment can proceed, must pause, or belongs elsewhere.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-conflict and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when disability-access, health, subsidy, or tenant-selection policies conflict with authoritative guidance or require authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Income, public-assistance, homelessness, foster-care, and subsidy authorization records
Selection criteria, waitlist, funded-slot, and vacancy records
Health, immunization, physical exam, developmental screening, and consent records
Emergency contact and pickup authorization forms
Provider licensing status, background-check, qualification, and training records
Intake disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: Child care CRM or application intake system
Provider licensing lookup and monitoring portals
Tenant work-queue and escalation tracking systems

allowed: Validate intake readiness, eligibility evidence, authorization state, and minimum provider or slot readiness before downstream work starts.
Prepare evidence-backed acceptance, pend, rejection, or routing notes without overstating approval authority.
Require child-care-specific records and systems context so the lane does not degrade into generic admissions handling.
Escalate health, disability, subsidy, legal, or policy conflicts instead of improvising enrollment outcomes.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction, program model, subsidy path, waitlist rule, or licensing context that changes the answer.
Conflicting public and tenant rules on priority populations, health-record timing, disability access, or funded-slot handling.
Requests involving clinical judgments, accommodation denials, legal interpretation, or subsidy-award authority.
refuse: Final subsidy award or payment approval decisions.
Clinical participation, accommodation sufficiency, or health-safety determinations outside the lane.
Enrollment advancement that skips identity, eligibility, authorization, referral, or provider-readiness checks.
Treating the child-care industry label as cosmetic and answering with generic admissions logic.

domain_failure_modes: A child can be offered a start date without funded-slot, waitlist, or subsidy authorization evidence, creating downstream service and payment failure.
Generic enrollment logic can miss child-care-specific records such as pickup authorizations, immunization timing, provider licensing status, and room-capacity readiness.
A child with a disability can be wrongly denied based on assumptions if the lane does not force individualized-assessment routing.
Homelessness or foster-care priority rules can be lost if the lane treats all applicants as equivalent household-intake cases.
Provider readiness can be overstated if staff background-check or training blockers are not checked when placement depends on a specific site or room.
Conflicting state licensing, Head Start, and tenant rules can change whether a pending-record or waitlist path is allowed, so guessing creates false confidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Missing prerequisite or required intake data
Request routed to the wrong lane before execution starts
Attempt to bypass access, eligibility, or authorization checks

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Jurisdiction-specific licensing rules control the exact timing and form of immunization, health, and provider-readiness artifacts outside Head Start-specific pathways.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: May materially change whether a pending-record start is allowed or blocked.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Non-blocking because the pack requires jurisdiction and tenant policy retrieval before the lane finalizes any timing-sensitive acceptance.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Tenant selection criteria, sibling priority, subsidy connectors, and funded-slot accounting differ by operator and program mix.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Can change acceptance, pend, and routing behavior even when public baseline sources are stable.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Non-blocking because the lane explicitly requires tenant retrieval and escalates instead of inferring missing policy.

notes: The lane is intake-only: it validates readiness, packages evidence, and routes ownership without becoming the final subsidy, health, placement, or legal approver.
validated status remains supportable because unresolved items are handled through required tenant retrieval and refusal rather than silent synthesis.
